content: 【<DevouredIconKeywords:吞噬>：使其他唤醒体获得 <Energy:[Energy:Arg1]> 点狂气。】对所有敌人造成 <Damage:[Damage:Arg2]> 点伤害，造成 [Arg3]% 伤害的<BleedingIconKeywords:出血>，并施加 [Arg4]% 伤害的<IntoxicationIconKeywords:中毒>并获得 [Arg5]% 伤害的<RetaliateIconKeywords:反击>。选择 1 张手中的非衍生指令卡，将其 2 张附加<DepleteIconKeywords:消耗>和<NothingnessIconKeywords:虚无>的原始复制置入手中，使其算力消耗 -[Arg6]。接下来 3 回合内，每回合开始时将 1 张「胚胎」转化为「圣洁之子」，若没有「胚胎」则将 1 张「胚胎」置入手中。
Yes
Thais Exalt states "非衍生指令卡" (literally translates to not Derived command card)
and we have a whole bunch of terms that goes "not derived card, not exhaust card, not void card" in Chinese

More testing needs to be done to confirm this but if the translation for the term really is supposed to be "Non-Derived" then this may be a bug. 
Is the part of the term 非衍生指令卡 here that means "Derived" the exact same word that appears at the bottom of Derived Command cards?
and turns out we've only translated 非衍生 into Innate, while all the others have stayed "Non-Exhaust, Non-Void", and people complained. So we've been changing the translation of 非衍生 into Non-Derived

If so I will rally the playtesters I've gathered to help identify all of the effects that copy or target "Innate/Non-Derived" cards that CAN'T copy or target (for example) the card objects Thais Exalt creates.
because I have a very firm memory of noticing at least one of those effects behaving that way but didn't write it down so I can't say which one it was.

ah yes, so like

固有 means a card always shows up in your hand at the start of the turn. It is now translated into Innate. And I agree it should stay Innate now;

非衍生 is a whole another thing, it simply means this card isn't "created by" or "derived from" another card. 

but I vaguely remember the combat designers talking about some exceptions in Thais's case like some cards are supposed to be "created" by another card but can still be copied by thais for whatever reason. <:aurita_dizzy:1408324284702654556>
I think non-derived works but it's a mouthful

I guess I'll just make sure 固有 is always translated into Innate, 
and unify the translation of 非衍生 into Non-Derived for the time being!

In case Non-Derived should indeed be changed into something else I'll let you guys know asap. Unifying it now would also make the later potential change easier to execute
Wonderful, thank you so much.
We should be thanking you guys!!

In the community discussions I'm having, I was referring to this term (非衍生 if I'm understanding correctly) as "Class A" cards (until we all agreed on Primary) and was referring to this other problematic undefined card attribute as "Class C" cards; those being, cards that are "created by" or "derived from" other effects and ARE NOT **Derived Command cards**
standardizing 非衍生 to "Non-Derived" is good progress forward but would inevitably then bump into the problem of the word "Derived" then being used by two different definitions...
combat designers weren't lying they still need to do a lot of work on standardizing the CN descriptions
I really wish I understood Chinese so that I could help! 😭

I suggested when I was speaking to Light that Lens and I team up for that since Light asked if I could help on that front but I think it being secondary to the discussion means it got overlooked and I didn't get a response about it

DW you've already been a big help!
I remember Light and Mijiu (the lead combat designer) were excited about your suggestions and they discussed a lot. 

Light may have been too busy with the anni stuff so he haven't looked at DMs again yet. 

It's also possible he didn't even realize you've been waiting for a response because he uses translator to read english message
i'm also guilty of late replies<:lmfao:1408867314497228941>  i'm compiling our discussion about 非衍生 for them rn
I'm not currently waiting for a reply from Light, I supposed the discussion was over after the relevant parts of the discussion were concluded.
but yeah I would love to open dialogue again with him after Anniversary with some suggestions for the game!

-# *(one of which being that the resonance buff cards should be unique objects for each resonance combination instead of frankenstein-ing components of sentences together as individual strings of text)* 😅

this other problematic undefined card attribute as "Class C" cards; those being, cards that are "created by" or "derived from" other effects and ARE NOT Derived Command cards

Do you mind providing an example of this?
Ah sorry I may have misread your message

I was going to ask about Class A;

非衍生 simply means a card that is not a derived command card (not copied or created from a Base Command Card.

So it would seem 非衍生=Class C you mentioned...

But what kind of cards were classfied as "Class A"?

Before I say anything more I'm going to do a little testing to see if I can nail down a proper definition real quick. You mentioned Thais Exalt being some kind of exception and for some reason I'm struggling to provide another example so bear with me for a moment

Ok!!

Also I just got back from combat designer:

Turns out they may still want to design some more stuff where Exhaust and Void(Fleeting) takes effects separately, so he asked us to not use Fleeting to imply both for the time being, but they are considering an eventual merge (not sure when).

Also, we do indeed need to make sure <EmptinessKeywords:空虚> and <NothingnessIconKeywords:虚无> are translated into different words.

So we'll do this:

A. Change <NothingnessIconKeywords:虚无> from **Void** to **Fleeting**, and <EmptinessKeywords:空虚> will stay as **Void**.
We'll keep using **"Exhaust and Fleeting"** to keep them separate. 

Or maybe we could:
B.  Change <NothingnessIconKeywords:虚无> from **Void** to **Phemeral**.
Because Mijiu said 虚无 is based on Phemeral in Legends of Runeterra;

This way we can save Fleeting for the new term that means "Exhaust +  <NothingnessIconKeywords:虚无>", when the merge happens in CN

I haven't played Legends of Runeterra, but I do know the term for this in Slay the Spire is "Ethereal".
I dislike Ephemeral and Ethereal both here and would much prefer Fleeting if possible.
If there is an eventual third Keyword defined that means "Exhaust + Fleeting", then Ephemeral would work for that very well but I wouldn't use Ethereal.
Ephemereal is moreso LoR's version of Exhaust TBH
it's only on unit cards that die after attacking or at the end of the turn
I see then we'll go with A?
LoR also uses Fleeting for reference
https://leagueoflegends.fandom.com/wiki/Keywords_(Legends_of_Runeterra)/Fleeting

embeds.title: Fleeting
embeds.description: Fleeting is a keyword in Legends of Runeterra. EditCards with Fleeting discard when the round ends.Fleeting is removed from a unit when it is summoned. PENDING FOR TEST: How does this interact...

content:
okay so here's some stuff I've gathered.
For these examples I will be using these terms: 
"Class A card" : "Primary"
"Class C card" : "Class C"
"Derived Command card" : "Derived Command"
Here's Helot: Catena's card 「Bloodthirsty Flail」; observe Thais Exalt being able to copy it, and the Posse "Lone Seed" able to copy both it and the Class C copies of it created by Thais Exalt.
(more examples to come)

Here's Doresain's Derived Command card 「Evernight's Revel」 (note at the bottom of the card its card type, Derived Command, and note Bloodthirsty Flail did not have this card type)
Observe neither Thais Exalt or Lone Seed being able to copy it.

Here's a Class C version of Ramona's Primary (Class A) card 「Queen's Sword」 created by the Wheel of Destiny "Chains Unbound". Observe Lone Seed being able to copy it, and Thais Exalt being able to copy both it and the Class C copy of it created by Lone Seed.

I would describe Class A cards here as "Non-Derived". I'm still in favor of describing them as "Primary", but so far as testing has confirmed, "Non-Derived" is an accurate description. More testing will be done.

Class C cards here have no clear definition, and until I can find evidence for what effect it was that made me think there was a distinction here, I give you permission to believe I'm off my goddamn rocker and proceed with your day

I WILL however note that the "Derived being used in two different ways" thing here is not just the way you're describing it, but also the label of the Tooltip. Refer to the first image of the first post, where the Tooltip describing "Crimson Shackles" (a Class A Command card) is titled "Derived Card Description".
I rest my case for now and as it's 2am I'm gonna go to bed good night

Hello guys!

We'll be posting October's contribution list today!
All of October's volunteer translations are in-game now;

btw we may need to temporarily the text in "MiscErrors" file you uploaded in an upcoming hotfix, because we just received complaint about a translation error in there;
This should be "inflict" not "trigger" Poison, and 生命 should be HP. So the older version of this translation was correct.
You don't need to do anything on your end! We missed this error in your translation and it's on us; we'll re-proofread your translation and put them back in game asap.

<@88644018673582080>
yes sorry! I noticed this as well and I think Mercy should have a pending fix to change it: https://discord.com/channels/1402537664619479100/1402538040253087766/1429343410321494038
that one is my fault, I did not have the wheel myself and changed it based on a q&a thread feedback: https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1408434437049417910/1409287597133529298

I see! Ty for letting us know!!
I'm asking light when the next hotfix is happening and we'll fix this & incorporate Mercy's fix in that update
but it turns out the person who posted that q&a thread was wrong about it as well....
it happens! It's ultimately our responsibility to keep the translation consistent. Sorry we failed to do that and caused all this confusion. Thank you guys so much for your help

I don't have any pending changes right now, I've been busy with a lot of other things. I was given the OK to do my overhaul so I'm less worried about small errors like that that will end up nearly completely replaced when I'm done, but it sounds like Ace has this figured out...? I'm confused by this whole situation so I just wanted to clarify that I've done nothing and plan to do nothing about this particular problem until I upload a full optimization of ALL WoDs
oh sorry, I thought you already did it
<@1376763322568937562> I misunderstood, Mercy doesn't have a pending fix so you can fix it
sorry <:aurita_dizzy:1408324284702654556>

what I DO have is all of Act 1's Resonances, a second pass on Event dialogs, and a change to fix all erroneous occurences of "Consumes" to the correct "Exhaust", and change "Void" to "Fleeting" (except in the case of Aliemus Void)

but I don't think it's fair at this point since I missed the deadline for this update to provide my work without also providing all the other work that went through my repo, so I still have to compile everything before I can present it.
I see!
About "Void" -> "Fleeting": Is it because you find "Fleeting" would be easier to understand?

At this point we believe we won't be able to incorporate that change because "Void" has been a long standing term and there doesn't seem to be a reason substantial enough to support this change. 
But we're also open to your thoughts on why it would be necessary;
Like, we're mainly worried that people would feel confused as we've put "Void" on a lot of official character kit description images...

I've been discussing this for quite a while now, and everyone appears to be on-board with the change. EN players will be happy to see changes toward the better, so long as they're implemented in whole, which I take my time do my absolute best to make sure of.

First, "Void" is currently a word used for *two* very different effects and never should the same word be keyworded to two different things. This appears to be because MTL translated both 虚无 and 空虚 to Void, when 虚无 (the Void in question) means a card will Exhaust if left in your hand at end of turn, and 空虚 (the Void that will remain Void) means that Awakeners will gain less Aliemus.

Second, Void as a verb is quite awkward. It doesn't properly insinuate the effect we're trying to describe, and Fleeting does that quite well. 
Third, Fleeting able to function as both a verb and an adjective allows it to, in most cases, **imply** "Exhaust" in some effects to cut down on text; 
> Create a copy of a Command card in your hand. It has Exhaust and Fleeting.
vs
> Create a Fleeting copy of a Command card in your hand.
I've fielded opinions on this in some community discussions and had no one argue against it so far, so I've been proceeding with this and will put it in my EN Style Guide when it's finished.

I would argue this is a *very* substantial issue with robust reasoning behind the decision and I really do hope it's possible to make this change. Past content like promotional images that can't be changed are fated to be outdated regardless, that's just a side-effect of retranslation work and can't be avoided. Future content that may not align correctly with current style standards is *already* a problem as the first step of the process is MTL anyway, so keeping up with this change seems minor, especially since it's been established that it's possible to instruct the MTL to translate specific words a specific way;

<:void:1414485025579335730>空虚 `<EmptinessKeywords:空虚>` should stay "Void" or "Aliemus Void"
<:warn:1408311425952387122>虚无 `<NothingnessIconKeywords:虚无>` should be translated to "Fleeting".
Please don't make final changes to the style guide yet

Sorry if I was unclear, I meant **MY** style guide, that I'm writing behind the scenes as I make changes to help future translations of the game stay consistent. I haven't shared any version of it yet and don't intend to until it's done, and I'm not making changes to any of your style guides.
I did recently realize that the EN-Morimens Term Base google sheets has volunteer suggestion fields so I put a few things down there, but I only changed the "Suggested Change", "Notes" and "Contributor" columns. My bad if I wasn't supposed to edit that document, I just did what I saw Arkaether and others do.
Understood! I've translated and forwarded your message to Combat Designer & Light

Turns out they have considered **combining Exhaust and Void into one single turn** as you've mentioned too because they very often show up together;

if it happens then we may change the Chinese og desc too? Please give us some time to consider!

Interesting! Much testing and searching has confirmed that the ONLY effect in the game that presents Void without Exhaust is the relic **Hartford's Elixir** which gives your Symptom cards Void at the start of each battle, but not Exhaust.

If it's possible to change that relic's effect and then make Fleeting *always* include or imply Exhaust within its effect that would be AMAZING 🤩
Like a dream come true
thank you for the additional info!

I have another relevant suggestion that isn't necessarily translation related but would be a great benefit to the readability of the game as a whole but wanted to wait until after Anniversary to propose it because I understand Anniversary is a very busy time for any game dev, so I don't want to add something more to Light's already full plate.

Since we're on the topic though the idea was to make commonly used affixes & attributes into UI elements on cards instead of words inside the text box. I'm gonna let the idea simmer and present it fully thought-out with some visual examples of my thoughts when it comes time.
Noted!

OH also actually relevant to the discussion is another situation exactly like the Void situation but way worse

The term "Innate" ALSO is being used for two very different things, but it's a much more important problem because both things refer to an attribute of a card.

"Innate" is a keyword that means a card is guaranteed in your opening hand for each battle, and
Innate appears to be the go-to term to refer to a card that began the battle in your deck.

This is a huge problem because things that refer to "Innate Command cards" do not actually refer to Command cards that have Innate. I again have had a lot of discussion with the community on the matter and have come to a solid consensus that this term should instead be "Primary", and I've been going forward from that for the Act 1 Resonances work.

if it helps to put it in context, here's the example I used for much of the discussion I've been having on the topic:

The way I would template the end result of Thais Exalt when I get to Awakener Kits would be:
> [<:warn:1408311425952387122>Devour: Grant all other Awakeners `N` Aliemus.] 
> Deal `X` DMG to all enemies. On each enemy, inflict `X`% of that DMG as <:bleed:1408309205722595329>Bleed and `X`% of that DMG as <:poison:1408309181496299593>Poison. Gain <:counter:1408311255562977375>Counter equal to `X`% of the total DMG dealt.
> Choose a Primary Command card in hand; create 2 basic, Fleeting copies of it with their Arithmetica Costs reduced by 2.

Non-Derived is a mouthful and I'm glad it got changed at all but I have significant concerns about using the name of a Card Type (Derived Command) for this description as well **because a lot of these effects cannot target Command cards that are a copy of another card.** Referring to "Non-Derived Command cards" and having the player be unable to target one of their Command cards will cause more confusion than it's worth.

I warned Arkaether/Lens about this change when he submitted it, referring to it as "putting lipstick on a pig" but it appears to be in the game now and hopefully it's not off the table to change it to be more clear later on.

So we'll be doing this:

1. "固有指令卡" (meaning a card is guaranteed to show up in your hand at the start of battle) will be translated into **Primary** Command Card, instead of **Innate**

2. "非衍生指令卡" will be translated into **Non-Derived** Command Card, instead of **Innate**
This way it's easier to understand and follows the same pattern of "非消耗", "非虚无"
I don't like using Primary to imply card that starts in your hand
nonono that's even worse because Innate is a very established keyword
Innate is as integral to the Spirelike genre as Exhaust is
two different things are both translated into Innate now and we need to differenciate?
https://slay-the-spire.fandom.com/wiki/Innate

embeds.title: Innate
embeds.description: Innate is a keyword which causes the card to always start in your opening hand. Technically, it works as if cards with Innate are on the top of your draw pile before your first draw, thus meaning...

content: I think it has to be Innate
Yes, a card being guaranteed in your opeing hand should ALWAYS say "Innate"
the OTHER ("Non-Derived") term should be Primary
I'm so sorry if I confused you

Ahhh... we received complaints about Innate being a whole new word that doesn't contain "Derived", despite the Chinese version of it containing "Derived" (衍生), so we've been phasing out Innate for Non-Derived
I think Innate should stay as a term for cards that start in your hand, it's an easily recognizable term for anyone with remote familiarity with the genre

so 固有=Innate; 非衍生= Primary/Non-Derived...?

I'm still inclined to have Non-Derived because we have a whole bunch of "Non-Exhaust", "Non-Void"...
so you're saying the **Chinese word** for the kind of cards that Thais Exalt can target contains "Non-Derived"?
非衍生，非消耗，非虚无etc
